name: Register Ecosystem Capabilities (PR-F2, handover F-010)

design_notes.nature_of_this_feature: Handover F-010. Register the ecosystem lifecycle capabilities across the three routing surfaces ecosystem/README.md's own 'Capability Routing' section already names: NIZAM.json, tools/skill.json, tools/SKILL.md. Per feature_list_005.json feature 040 acceptance core: (1) 'NIZAM.json paths resolve' (C4 green); (2) 'tools/skill.json routes capabilities'; (3) 'tools/SKILL.md remains a router rather than a mirror'. Registration is scoped STRICTLY to the four SHIPPED ecosystem protocol documents -- ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md (033), ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md (034), ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md (035), ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md (036) -- per the module's own Shipped/Planned table (ecosystem/README.md Sec 'Module Navigation'), which explicitly warns against directory-qualifying a not-yet-existing protocol document to avoid a dangling reference. The five other numbered protocols (00, 04, 05, 06, 08) are Planned, do not exist in this repository, and are NOT registered here -- registering them would immediately break 'NIZAM.json paths resolve' (C4/C9) and is explicitly out of this feature's scope. This feature does NOT author any ecosystem protocol prose (033/034/035/036's shipped scope, or later features' scope for the Planned documents), does NOT add any new schema (037/038/039's scope), and does NOT introduce any runtime-specific ecosystem skill -- ecosystem/README.md's own routing section is explicit that 'the single, runtime-agnostic skill remains the router for every ecosystem capability; no runtime-specific ecosystem skills are introduced', and this feature's own non_goals and scope-guard enforce exactly that.
design_notes.capability_design: Three routing surfaces, each with its own registration granularity, all four SHIPPED protocols registered on EVERY surface (dual/multi-surface indexing -- per the 037/039 lesson that if a capability is registered in multiple files, EVERY file gets an instance-level exact check, not a vacuous substring): (a) NIZAM.json 'modules' array, the ecosystem module's own 'key_documents' list -- extended from ['ecosystem/README.md'] to append the four shipped protocol paths, mirroring the schema module's 'key_documents' precedent (037/038/039) of listing every governed document under that module; (b) NIZAM.json top-level 'capabilities' array -- four new capability objects (id/summary/authoritative_source), one per shipped protocol, following the EXACT granularity precedent the standard/methodology/tools modules already establish: every substantive protocol document in those three modules has its own capability entry, but each module's own README.md (navigational, not a capability) does NOT -- 11 of 12 standard/*.md key_documents have a capability entry (README excluded), 9 of 10 methodology/*.md key_documents have one (README excluded), 4 of 5 tools/* key_documents have one (README excluded). This feature deliberately follows the identical convention: ecosystem/README.md (navigational module index) gets NO new capability entry of its own; only the four shipped PROTOCOL documents do; (c) tools/skill.json 'capabilities' array -- four new entries {name, description, module}, mirroring the shape and one-per-substantive-document granularity of its ten pre-existing entries (which similarly mirror a curated subset of standard/methodology capabilities, never every one -- this feature adds a complete set for ecosystem specifically, since acceptance criterion 2 explicitly requires tools/skill.json to route ecosystem capabilities, and ecosystem/README.md's own routing section names tools/skill.json by name as a required routing surface). tools/SKILL.md is the fourth surface but is prose, not a structured array -- see router_vs_mirror_design.
design_notes.router_vs_mirror_design: tools/SKILL.md gains one new numbered section (new Section 8, 'Ecosystem Engineering Cycle Routing'; the pre-existing Section 8 'References' is renumbered to Section 9 -- verified no other section or cross-reference in the document names 'Section 8' internally, so the renumbering is safe) that: names ecosystem/README.md as the module's own authoritative index; states this skill remains the single router for the ecosystem module too (no runtime-specific ecosystem skill); and lists the four shipped protocol paths as backtick-delimited routing targets, each with only a two-to-four-word lifecycle-stage label (Preflight/Baseline/Audit/Compare) -- NOT a restated definition of what each protocol's rules actually are. This mirrors Section 1's own existing style (a bulleted list of WHEN-to-load triggers, never restating the protocol bodies) rather than Section 3's style (which summarizes -- but does not reproduce verbatim -- methodology/01_execution.md's mechanics). The objective, non-vacuous router-not-mirror proof now has FOUR independent legs (widened from three per .agent/qa/040-contract-review.json's HIGH finding: a substantive, non-verbatim paraphrase of all four protocols' operative rules was shown to pass the original three legs at 25 added lines, close in order of magnitude to the honest design's own line count, proving an aggregate line-count cap alone cannot structurally separate 'router' from 'mirror' content): (1) content-completeness -- each of the four paths actually appears, backtick-delimited (not a vacuous file-level grep for 'ecosystem'); (2) mirror-prevention -- none of the four protocols' single most distinctive, exact, load-bearing sentence appears verbatim in tools/SKILL.md (each such sentence is unique among the four ecosystem/*.md protocol bodies and absent from tools/SKILL.md -- see the corrected, narrower claim in dry_run_proof; one of the four is also separately present in docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md, which is immaterial since this check only inspects tools/SKILL.md); (3) a line-count cap on the net lines added to tools/SKILL.md by this feature's own diff, bounding gross bloat (a full mirror of even one 60-100-line protocol Section would blow the 40-line cap); and (4) STRUCTURAL WHOLE-SECTION CLOSED-SET WITH AN EXACT-TEXT-PINNED PREAMBLE -- widened a THIRD time, architecturally, per .agent/qa/040-contract-review-rev2.json's HIGH finding: the second widening's own bounded preamble (<=2 lines, <=90 characters each, no other constraint) was itself demonstrated to be a free-text canvas -- two independently-constructed 2-line digests (170 and 157 characters) synthesized all four protocols' operative rules together, one reproducing the literal preflight verdict enum tokens PASS/PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS/FAIL verbatim, and both passed every existing leg (a length-only bound, as round 0 already proved for bullets, cannot separate router content from mirror content at any threshold). The architectural fix eliminates free text from the preamble ENTIRELY: the preamble is pinned, byte-for-byte, to an exact two-line string committed in design_notes.pinned_preamble_text, and entry 13 asserts list equality against it, not a length or word bound. Because the pinned text itself names ecosystem/README.md as the module's authoritative index and states this skill routes rather than reproduces, the same byte-compare mechanism also closes the evaluator's separate, lower-severity missing_acceptance_coverage finding (the preamble's required positive content was previously asserted only in design_notes prose, never independently verified). After this fix, the ONLY text in Section 8 an implementation can still choose is each of the four bullets' own <=40-character/<=6-word label -- the residual the evaluator itself explicitly judged non-blocking in its round-1 review, for reasons that remain valid (per-bullet-isolated, regex-shape-constrained, no exact protocol vocabulary demonstrated to fit, categorically smaller payload than a synthesized multi-protocol digest).
design_notes.concurrency_fence: Feature 039 (Engineering Finding Schema + Fixtures, handover F-009) is in evaluator review at the time this contract is drafted and is serialized to IMPLEMENT AND COMMIT before this feature implements -- its contracted surfaces are schema/engineering_finding.schema.json (new), six tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_*.json fixtures (new), NIZAM.json's top-level 'schemas' array and the 'schema' module's 'key_documents' array (both distinct JSON paths from anything this feature touches), and schema/README.md. This feature's own NIZAM.json edits are confined to the 'modules' array's ecosystem entry and a strict append to the top-level 'capabilities' array (verification entry 5's structural-diff check asserts exactly this, HEAD-relative, so it is correct whether run before or after 039 lands -- it does not assert anything about the 'schemas' array or the 'schema' module, which 039 owns). This feature does not touch schema/engineering_finding.schema.json, any tools/fixtures/* file, or schema/README.md, and its non_goals and scope-guard enforce that. Per explicit orchestrator instruction, .agent/run_state.json and the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md are fenced: this feature authorizes no edit to either (both remain non-goals, consistent with the 037/038/039 precedent of tolerating v0.6.0-release-notes.md in the scope-guard allow-list without asserting it stays byte-identical, and never gating on .agent/run_state.json's content since it is orchestrator-owned and legitimately mutated by routine bookkeeping outside any single feature's contract).
design_notes.dry_run_proof: Every mechanism below was proven against SCRATCH COPIES ONLY: (1) a bare python-edited copy of the real NIZAM.json (ecosystem key_documents extended to 5 entries, capabilities extended from 24 to 28 entries) still validates against a scratch copy of registry/nizam-index.schema.json (jsonschema.validate, zero errors). (2) A full, git-inclusive disposable clone of the entire repository tree (cp -a, .git included, outside the repository) with the three edited files copied in: `bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json` exits 0 with '[C4] PASS index-integrity', and the FULL `bash tools/validate.sh` sweep (all eleven checks, including C1 frontmatter-schema and C8 version-bump-vs-changelog against the edited tools/SKILL.md) prints 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed' -- proving the SKILL.md frontmatter/version/change_log edits are C1/C8-clean, not merely schema-clean in isolation. (3) The HEAD copy of the real NIZAM.json (git show HEAD:NIZAM.json) does not contain any of the four new capability ids or the four new key_documents paths -- anti-vacuity confirmed live. (4) The HEAD copy of tools/skill.json does not contain any of the four new 'module' path strings -- anti-vacuity confirmed live. (5) The HEAD copy of tools/SKILL.md does not contain any of the four new path strings -- anti-vacuity confirmed live. (6) None of the four protocols' single distinctive sentence ('No other value is a valid preflight verdict'; 'A baseline MUST NOT mix evidence from unspecified revisions'; 'No claim may be promoted beyond its evidence'; 'Every score movement between the two compared executions is traceable to') appears in the edited scratch tools/SKILL.md -- confirmed by direct python substring search. CORRECTED per .agent/qa/040-contract-review.json's unsupported_claims finding: these four sentences are each unique AMONG THE ECOSYSTEM PROTOCOL BODIES (one hit apiece, in exactly one of the four ecosystem/*.md files) and are, as claimed, absent from tools/SKILL.md -- but the ORIGINAL claim that they are unique repository-wide was false and is withdrawn: `grep -rln -- "No claim may be promoted beyond its evidence" ecosystem tools schema standard methodology docs registry NIZAM.json templates` independently re-confirms this sentence also appears in docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md (the accepted handover proposal, which quotes the protocol). This pre-existing duplication is immaterial to verification entry 14's mechanics (it only inspects tools/SKILL.md's own text), but the narrower, accurate claim is: unique among the four ecosystem/*.md protocol bodies, and absent from tools/SKILL.md specifically -- not unique across the whole repository. (7) `git diff --numstat HEAD -- tools/SKILL.md` on the disposable clone reports 22 lines added, 2 removed -- comfortably under the cap enforced by verification entry 15. (8) A structural diff of the edited NIZAM.json against its own HEAD copy (python, dict-level) confirms every top-level key except 'modules' and 'capabilities' is unchanged, every module object except the 'ecosystem' entry is byte-for-byte unchanged, the ecosystem entry's 'key_documents' gained exactly the four expected paths, and 'capabilities' gained exactly a four-entry, order-preserving, non-mutating append -- run against the disposable clone's real HEAD, so it is provably non-vacuous and 039-tolerant by construction (039 owns 'schemas' and the 'schema' module only, both left provably untouched by this diff). (9) `source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'NIZAM.json' 'tools/skill.json' 'tools/SKILL.md' 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md'` returns PASS on the disposable clone with the three edits applied and the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md and .agent/contracts/039.json present (the latter is invisible to vlib_scope_guard by construction -- it always excludes '.agent'). (10) The two-part .agent/ immutability guard (mirroring 038 amendment_2's invariant, glob range widened to 03[4-9]/04[0-5] per the current phase-005 in-flight range) returns PASS on the disposable clone with both .agent/contracts/039.json and a touch-created .agent/contracts/040.json present as untracked additions. Nothing under the real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework was created, edited, or deleted to produce any of the above; all edits were applied to python-scripted copies or a fully disposable `cp -a` clone under the session scratchpad directory. (11) FOUR ADVERSARIAL MUTATION PROBES confirm the least-trivial checks above are load-bearing, not vacuous: injecting the '01' protocol's distinctive sentence into a scratch SKILL.md correctly fails the entry-14 mirror-prevention check; bloating the scratch SKILL.md router section with 45 filler lines correctly fails the entry-15 line-cap check (67 > 40); tampering an unrelated NIZAM.json top-level field (framework.version) correctly fails the entry-5 structural-scope check; and tampering one capability's authoritative_source to a dangling path correctly fails the entry-4 exact-membership check. (12) The disposable clone's own .agent/contracts/040.json placeholder was initially created via a bare `touch` (0 bytes) while proving the .agent/ two-part immutability guard in isolation; this correctly broke tools/validate.sh's C11 dogfood check (which parses every .agent/contracts/*.json as JSON), confirming C11 does inspect this feature's own contract artifact -- replacing the placeholder with this contract's real, well-formed JSON content restored 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed', and ALL 25 verification commands in this contract's own 'verification' array were then executed VERBATIM (via a script iterating the JSON, not retyped) against the disposable clone with the three real edits applied, and all 25 returned exit 0 with PASS. REVISION 1 ADDENDUM (per .agent/qa/040-contract-review.json's HIGH finding on entries 12/14/15): a fourth, structural per-bullet anti-paraphrase check was added and re-proven against three fixtures on the disposable clone. (a) The honest, label-only design (this contract's own intended tools/SKILL.md edit, four one-line bullets each reading '- `<path>` -- <ShortLabel>.') PASSES the new check -- each bullet is a single physical line matching the required shape, each trailing label is 1 word / <=10 characters, well under the 6-word/40-character cap, and the line immediately following each bullet is either another bullet or blank. (b) A substantive, non-verbatim PARAPHRASE-MIRROR fixture was built, restating each of the four protocols' single most operative rule (Preflight's three-verdict/operator-exception gate; Baseline's single-revision-anchoring rule; Audit's confidence-cannot-exceed-evidence rule; Compare's per-finding traceability rule) across 2-3 physical lines per bullet -- `git diff --numstat` measured 30 lines added (same order of magnitude as the evaluator's own 25-line fixture and the honest design's 16-22 lines); this fixture PASSES both pre-existing legs (the verbatim-canary check, since no exact sentence is reproduced; and the 40-line aggregate cap, since 30<40) but correctly FAILS the new structural check on all four bullets, each flagged for 'a non-blank, non-bullet continuation line' immediately following the bullet. (c) A minimal two-physical-line stretch of a SINGLE otherwise-honest bullet (short first line unchanged, a second physical line 'See Section 3 for the general shape.' appended beneath it, with no bullet marker) was built in isolation -- this, too, correctly FAILS the new structural check (continuation-line violation on that one bullet), proving the mechanism does not merely key off content length or paraphrase density but off physical-line-count-per-bullet directly. All three fixtures were discarded scratch artifacts under the session scratchpad directory; nothing in the real repository was touched. REVISION 2 ADDENDUM (per .agent/qa/040-contract-review-rev1.json's HIGH + MEDIUM findings, independently converged on by Validator Mode A, at the real, current, committed HEAD 3aed5d1 -- 039 fully committed): entry 13 was widened from a per-bullet-only check into a whole-section closed-set check (heading + <=2-line bounded preamble, each line <=90 characters, + exactly 4 contracted bullets in order + nothing else) with an explicit exact-cardinality assertion. Re-proven on a fresh disposable clone at HEAD 3aed5d1 against SIX required scenarios: (a) the honest, redesigned Section 8 (2-line preamble, 15 net added lines, no trailing sentence after the bullets) PASSES; (b) Validator Mode A's free-prose paraphrase shape (a full paragraph restating all four protocols' rules, placed immediately after the four bullets, 26 net added lines -- same class as Mode A's 22-line fixture) FAILS, flagged on every non-blank paragraph line as 'found after bullets began'; (c) the evaluator's own shorter 21-line variant of the same attack (a terser 'In short: ...' paragraph after the bullets, matching the evaluator's reported 18-line class) FAILS identically; (d) a fifth, unauthorized bullet ('- `ecosystem/README.md` -- Overview.') appended after the four compliant ones FAILS the exact-cardinality assertion ('expected exactly 4 bullets, found 5'); (e) a combined probe -- one bullet's label bloated to 10 words / 65 characters (both over their respective caps) AND a second physical continuation line appended beneath that same bullet -- FAILS on all three grounds simultaneously (continuation-line violation, word-cap violation, char-cap violation); (f) two preamble-bound probes both FAIL: a third preamble line appended after the honest 2-line preamble ('preamble exceeds 2 lines'), and a single preamble line independently lengthened past 90 characters ('preamble line exceeds 90 chars (170)'). All six probes were discarded scratch artifacts under the session scratchpad directory; the full `bash tools/validate.sh` sweep against the honest edit alone was re-confirmed 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed' at the same HEAD. Nothing in the real repository was touched. REVISION 3 ADDENDUM (per .agent/qa/040-contract-review-rev2.json's HIGH finding, the final authorized negotiation round under the orchestrator's attempt-3 architectural-review ruling, at the real, current, committed HEAD 3aed5d1): the preamble was changed from a length bound to an exact-text byte-compare, and the honest tools/SKILL.md edit was updated to carry the pinned preamble text verbatim. Re-proven on a fresh disposable clone against FIVE required scenarios (the fourth split into two independent sub-fixtures per the evaluator's own two-variant evidence, and the fifth split into the two previously-closed-shape families plus a fresh between-bullets interleave the evaluator additionally demonstrated in its round-2 observations): (a) the honest design, with the pinned preamble applied verbatim, PASSES. (b) BOTH of the evaluator's own preamble-digest fixtures -- the 170-character variant reproducing the literal PASS/PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS/FAIL enum tokens, and the 157-character variant reproducing two of the three -- FAIL identically, each flagged 'preamble does not byte-match the pinned text'. (c) A minimal one-character deviation (a single trailing period appended to the pinned second line) FAILS on the same byte-compare -- proving the mechanism is a genuine byte-compare, not a normalized or fuzzy match. (d) Every previously-closed attack shape still fails: a trailing free-prose paragraph after all four bullets; a fresh variant placing prose BETWEEN two bullets (Preflight and Baseline) rather than after all four, mirroring the evaluator's own round-2 between-bullets observation; a fifth, unauthorized bullet (ecosystem/README.md); and a single bullet simultaneously stretched to two physical lines with a 10-word/65-character bloated label -- all FAIL, each for the same reasons proven in revision 2 (the byte-compare rewrite only replaced the preamble check; the bullet/cardinality/order logic is untouched and re-verified unchanged). (e) The pinned preamble text was independently inspected and confirmed to literally contain both 'ecosystem/README.md' and the word 'router', trivially satisfying the required-positive-content coverage the same byte-compare mechanism now also proves. The full `bash tools/validate.sh` sweep against the honest edit was re-confirmed 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed' at the same HEAD. All fixtures were discarded scratch artifacts under the session scratchpad directory; nothing in the real repository was touched.
design_notes.run_state_and_immutable_artifacts: This is a CONTRACT-PROPOSAL step (status=proposed): no implementation edit, no git commit, and no .agent/run_state.json rewrite happens under this document -- run_state.json is orchestrator-owned and batched centrally. Nothing under NIZAM.json, tools/, ecosystem/, schema/, or any other repo-tree surface was created, edited, or deleted to produce this proposal or its dry-run proofs; every proof ran against disposable scratch copies or a fully disposable full-repository clone outside the repository tree.
design_notes.pinned_preamble_text: The tools/SKILL.md Section 8 preamble is pinned, byte-for-byte, to exactly these two physical lines (verified by verification entry 13's exact list-equality assertion, not a length bound): Line 1: "This skill remains the sole router for the Ecosystem Engineering Cycle" (70 characters). Line 2: "module (`ecosystem/README.md`); it routes to, never reproduces, the protocols below:" (84 characters). This satisfies the evaluator's separate missing_acceptance_coverage finding (the preamble's required POSITIVE content -- naming ecosystem/README.md as the module's authoritative index, and asserting this skill routes rather than reproduces -- was previously asserted only in prose, never verified): because the preamble is now pinned to this exact text and the text itself contains both required assertions, the same byte-compare that prevents smuggling also trivially proves the required positive content is present, closing both findings with one mechanism.

files_modify.purpose: Extend the ecosystem module's key_documents array with the four SHIPPED ecosystem protocol paths (01/02/03/07), and append four new capability entries (one per shipped protocol) to the top-level capabilities array, mirroring the standard/methodology/tools modules' one-capability-per-substantive-document, README-excluded convention.

Append four new capability entries {name, description, module} to the capabilities array, one per shipped ecosystem protocol, each 'module' path resolving on disk; bump the top-level version field 0.1.1 -> 0.2.0.

Add a new Section 8 'Ecosystem Engineering Cycle Routing' (renumbering the pre-existing Section 8 'References' to Section 9) as a CLOSED SET with NO free text of any kind: a heading, an exact-text-pinned 2-line preamble (committed verbatim in design_notes; naming ecosystem/README.md as the module's own authoritative index and stating this skill routes to, never reproduces, the protocols below), and exactly four one-line bullets ('- `<path>` -- <label>') routing to the four shipped protocol paths in the contracted order -- the ONLY authorable text left in the section is each bullet's <=40-character/<=6-word label -- with no other content permitted anywhere in the section; bump frontmatter version 0.3.0 -> 0.4.0 with a matching change_log entry.

non_goals: No edit to ecosystem/README.md, ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md, ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md, ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md, or ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md -- protocol/module-index prose is out of this feature's scope (032/033/034/035/036's shipped scope).

No registration of any of the five Planned, not-yet-existing ecosystem protocol documents (00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md, 04_dependency_reconciliation.md, 05_release_train_coordination.md, 06_simplification_review.md, 08_ga_gate.md) in NIZAM.json, tools/skill.json, or tools/SKILL.md -- doing so would create a dangling path reference and break 'NIZAM.json paths resolve' (C4/C9); those are later features' or successor-phase scope.

No new capability entry for ecosystem/README.md itself -- mirrors the established README-exclusion convention already governing the standard, methodology, and tools modules' capability entries (navigational index documents are not individually registered as capabilities).

No creation of any runtime-specific ecosystem skill file (e.g. no ecosystem/skill.json, no ecosystem-scoped SKILL.md variant, no new tools/*skill* file besides the two authorized edits to the existing tools/skill.json and tools/SKILL.md) -- the single, runtime-agnostic skill remains the sole router per ecosystem/README.md's own Capability Routing section.

No creation or edit of schema/engineering_finding.schema.json, any tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_*.json fixture, or schema/README.md -- all are feature 039's concurrently in-flight (and serialized-to-land-first) scope; this feature's NIZAM.json edit is structurally confined (verification entry 5) to leave 039's 'schemas' array and 'schema' module surfaces untouched regardless of whether 039 has landed yet.
No creation or edit of tools/ecosystem_preflight.py, tools/compare_ecosystem_baselines.py, tools/validate_evidence_freshness.py, or any other deterministic ecosystem tooling -- feature 041's scope.
No edit to tools/interface.md, tools/validate.sh, tools/verify_lib.sh, tools/README.md, or any existing tools/fixtures/*.json fixture.

No edit to CHANGELOG.md -- CHANGELOG consolidation for phase 005 is the release feature's (045, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE) responsibility; this feature records its changes solely via NIZAM.json's own content, tools/skill.json's own version bump, and tools/SKILL.md's own frontmatter change_log, per the schema/README.md 0.3.0/0.4.0/0.5.0 precedent.
No edit to docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, or docs/planning/DEBT.md.

No edit to .agent/run_state.json -- orchestrator-owned, batched centrally, and legitimately mutated by routine bookkeeping outside this feature's own contract; not gated by any verification entry below for that reason (mirrors the 038/039 precedent).

No creation, edit, or deletion of the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md -- tolerated in the scope-guard allow-list solely to avoid a false-positive scope-guard failure, not because this feature is authorized to modify it.

No edit to any .agent/contracts/*.json other than this 040.json, or any .agent/qa/*.json -- those are immutable audit records or owned by the orchestrator/evaluator; feature 039's own concurrently in-flight .agent/contracts/039.json and .agent/qa/039*.json artifacts are tolerated as untracked additions, never asserted byte-identical (they are not this feature's scope to control).
No git commit of any kind is performed under this contract or its implementation step -- the deliverable is verified in the working tree; committing is a separate, later pipeline action.
No execution of any outstanding phase-005 human gate (H-FRAMEWORK-SCOPE, H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE, H-RISK) -- this feature's operator_gate is false.

No content in tools/SKILL.md's new Section 8 beyond the exact-text-pinned preamble (byte-for-byte, per design_notes.pinned_preamble_text -- not merely a bounded free-text preamble) and the exactly four contracted routing bullets in the contracted order -- no fifth bullet (including one routing to ecosystem/README.md, per the non-goal above), no free-standing prose paragraph before, between, or after the bullets, no deviation from the pinned preamble text by even a single character, and no bullet stretched across more than one physical line.

verification.acceptance_test: F-010/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): NIZAM.json stays C4-green with every indexed path resolving.
verification.command: bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json; test $? -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "--target NIZAM.json failed"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY (HEAD-anchored): the HEAD copy of NIZAM.json's ecosystem module key_documents contains none of the four new paths, and the HEAD capabilities array contains none of the four new capability ids -- proving the next two entries are non-vacuous.
verification.command: t=$(mktemp); git show HEAD:NIZAM.json > "${t}"; python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(open('${t}')); mod=[m for m in d['modules'] if m['path']=='ecosystem'][0]; new_paths=['ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md','ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md','ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md','ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md']; present=[p for p in new_paths if p in mod['key_documents']]; ids=[c['id'] for c in d['capabilities']]; new_ids=['nizam-ecosystem-preflight','nizam-ecosystem-baseline','nizam-ecosystem-audit','nizam-ecosystem-progress-comparison']; present_ids=[i for i in new_ids if i in ids]; sys.exit(1 if (present or present_ids) else 0)"; rc=$?; rm -f "${t}"; [ "${rc}" -eq 0 ] && echo PASS || { echo "NIZAM.json already carries one or more of the new paths/ids at HEAD -- would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: EXACT (not membership): the ecosystem module's key_documents array equals exactly ['ecosystem/README.md', 'ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md', 'ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md', 'ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md', 'ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md'], in that order.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('NIZAM.json')); mod=[m for m in d['modules'] if m['path']=='ecosystem'][0]; expected=['ecosystem/README.md','ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md','ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md','ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md','ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md']; assert mod['key_documents']==expected, mod['key_documents']" && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem module key_documents does not exactly match the expected 5-path list"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: EXACT (not membership): the capabilities array contains exactly one entry each for nizam-ecosystem-preflight/-baseline/-audit/-progress-comparison, each with the correct authoritative_source, and each authoritative_source path resolves on disk.

STRUCTURAL SCOPE (HEAD-relative, 039-tolerant): NIZAM.json's diff versus HEAD is confined to (a) the ecosystem module's key_documents field within 'modules' and (b) a strict, order-preserving, non-mutating append to the top-level 'capabilities' array -- every other top-level key and every other module object is byte-for-byte unchanged from HEAD, regardless of whatever HEAD state 039 has already landed.
verification.command:
python3 -c "import json,subprocess; head=json.loads(subprocess.check_output(['git','show','HEAD:NIZAM.json']).decode()); work=json.load(open('NIZAM.json'))
for k in set(head)|set(work):
  if k in ('modules','capabilities'): continue
  assert head.get(k)==work.get(k), ('top-level key changed out of scope', k)
hmods={m['path']:m for m in head['modules']}; wmods={m['path']:m for m in work['modules']}
assert set(hmods)==set(wmods), 'module path set changed'
for p in hmods:
  if p=='ecosystem': continue
  assert hmods[p]==wmods[p], ('non-ecosystem module changed', p)
added=[x for x in wmods['ecosystem']['key_documents'] if x not in hmods['ecosystem']['key_documents']]
expected_added=['ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md','ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md','ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md','ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md']
assert sorted(added)==sorted(expected_added), added
hcaps=head['capabilities']; wcaps=work['capabilities']
assert wcaps[:len(hcaps)]==hcaps, 'prior capability entries mutated or reordered'
new_ids=[c['id'] for c in wcaps[len(hcaps):]]
assert new_ids==['nizam-ecosystem-preflight','nizam-ecosystem-baseline','nizam-ecosystem-audit','nizam-ecosystem-progress-comparison'], new_ids" && echo PASS || { echo "NIZAM.json diff touches something outside the authorized modules.ecosystem + capabilities-append surface"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (defense-in-depth, direct schema validation): the edited NIZAM.json still validates against registry/nizam-index.schema.json.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; d=json.load(open('NIZAM.json')); s=json.load(open('registry/nizam-index.schema.json')); jsonschema.validate(d, s)" && echo PASS || { echo "NIZAM.json no longer validates against registry/nizam-index.schema.json"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-010/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): tools/skill.json parses and routes an ecosystem capability whose module path resolves on disk.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json,os,sys; s=json.load(open('tools/skill.json')); caps=s.get('capabilities',s); js=json.dumps(s); sys.exit(0 if 'ecosystem' in js.lower() else 1)" && echo PASS || { echo "tools/skill.json literal acceptance test failed"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY (HEAD-anchored): the HEAD copy of tools/skill.json's capabilities array contains no entry whose 'module' equals any of the four new paths.
verification.command: t=$(mktemp); git show HEAD:tools/skill.json > "${t}"; python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(open('${t}')); mods=[c.get('module') for c in d['capabilities']]; new_paths=['ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md','ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md','ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md','ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md']; present=[p for p in new_paths if p in mods]; sys.exit(1 if present else 0)"; rc=$?; rm -f "${t}"; [ "${rc}" -eq 0 ] && echo PASS || { echo "tools/skill.json already routes one of the new module paths at HEAD -- would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: EXACT (not membership): tools/skill.json's capabilities array gained exactly four new entries (name/description/module), one per shipped ecosystem protocol, each module path resolving on disk, appended after the pre-existing entries without mutating them.
ADDITIONAL: tools/skill.json remains valid, parseable JSON with a top-level 'capabilities' array.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('tools/skill.json')); assert isinstance(d.get('capabilities'), list) and len(d['capabilities'])>0" && echo PASS || { echo "tools/skill.json is not valid JSON or lacks a non-empty capabilities array"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL: tools/skill.json's top-level version field strictly increased over its HEAD value.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json,subprocess; head=json.loads(subprocess.check_output(['git','show','HEAD:tools/skill.json']).decode()); work=json.load(open('tools/skill.json')); hv=tuple(int(x) for x in head['version'].split('.')); wv=tuple(int(x) for x in work['version'].split('.')); assert wv>hv, (hv, wv)" && echo PASS || { echo "tools/skill.json version did not strictly increase"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-010/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json intent) + content-completeness: tools/SKILL.md names each of the four shipped ecosystem protocol paths as a literal, backtick-delimited routing reference.

STRUCTURAL WHOLE-SECTION CLOSED-SET, EXACT-TEXT-PINNED PREAMBLE (architectural fix per .agent/qa/040-contract-review-rev2.json's HIGH finding: revision 2's length-only-bounded preamble was itself a free-text canvas -- two independently-constructed 2-line preambles of 170 and 157 characters each digested all four protocols' rules, one reproducing the literal preflight verdict enum tokens verbatim, and both passed every prior leg): the preamble is now BYTE-COMPARED against the exact text pinned in design_notes.pinned_preamble_text, not merely length-bounded. Every non-blank line between the '## 8. Ecosystem Engineering Cycle Routing' heading and the next '## ' heading must be either (a) exactly the two pinned preamble lines, in order, byte-for-byte, (b) one of the four contracted bullets in the contracted order, matching '- `<path>` -- <label>' with the existing 6-word/40-character label cap, or (c) nothing else -- ANY deviation (a differing preamble, a fifth bullet, a stretched bullet, or a free paragraph anywhere in the section) fails. The pinned preamble text itself names ecosystem/README.md as the module's own authoritative index and states this skill routes to, never reproduces, the protocols -- so the same byte-compare that closes the smuggling vector also proves the preamble's required positive content is present (closing the evaluator's separate missing_acceptance_coverage finding). Also retains the exact-cardinality assertion (exactly 4 bullets, exactly the 4 contracted paths, no duplicates, no extras). After this fix, the ONLY authorable free text remaining anywhere in Section 8 is each bullet's own <=40-character label.
ANTI-VACUITY (HEAD-anchored): the HEAD copy of tools/SKILL.md contains none of the four shipped protocol paths.
verification.command: t=$(mktemp); git show HEAD:tools/SKILL.md > "${t}"; python3 -c "txt=open('${t}',encoding='utf-8').read(); paths=['ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md','ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md','ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md','ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md']; found=[p for p in paths if p in txt]; import sys; sys.exit(1 if found else 0)"; rc=$?; rm -f "${t}"; [ "${rc}" -eq 0 ] && echo PASS || { echo "tools/SKILL.md already names one of the four protocol paths at HEAD -- would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: MIRROR-PREVENTION, DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH (verbatim canary; the structural anti-paraphrase check elsewhere in this array is the primary defense against non-verbatim paraphrase): tools/SKILL.md does NOT contain any of the four protocols' single distinctive, exact, load-bearing sentence, verbatim.
verification.command: python3 -c "t=open('tools/SKILL.md',encoding='utf-8').read(); distinctive=['No other value is a valid preflight verdict','A baseline MUST NOT mix evidence from unspecified revisions','No claim may be promoted beyond its evidence','Every score movement between the two compared executions is traceable to']; bad=[d for d in distinctive if d in t]; assert not bad, bad" && echo PASS || { echo "tools/SKILL.md reproduces a distinctive protocol-body sentence verbatim -- it is mirroring, not routing"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: MIRROR-PREVENTION, DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH (aggregate bound; catches gross bloat -- e.g. reproducing whole protocol sections -- that the structural per-bullet check might not by itself bound as tightly): the net lines added to tools/SKILL.md by this feature's own diff does not exceed 40.
verification.command: added=$(git diff --numstat HEAD -- tools/SKILL.md | awk '{print $1}'); [ -n "${added}" ] && [ "${added}" -le 40 ] && echo PASS || { echo "tools/SKILL.md added ${added:-0} lines, exceeding the 40-line router-not-mirror cap"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL: tools/SKILL.md frontmatter still validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('tools/SKILL.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))" && echo PASS || { echo "tools/SKILL.md frontmatter failed schema validation"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL: tools/SKILL.md version-bump discipline (NDS Sec 7 / tools/validate.sh C8): the frontmatter version strictly increased over HEAD.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_version_increased tools/SKILL.md && echo PASS || { echo "tools/SKILL.md version did not strictly increase"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL: tools/SKILL.md's frontmatter change_log includes an entry whose version equals the bumped frontmatter version (satisfies C8 without touching CHANGELOG.md, per the non-goal above).
verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml; d=open('tools/SKILL.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; fm=yaml.safe_load(d); v=fm['version']; assert any(str(e.get('version'))==str(v) for e in (fm.get('change_log') or []))" && echo PASS || { echo "no matching change_log entry for the bumped tools/SKILL.md version"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-010/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): compliance validator stays green.
verification.command: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed' && echo PASS || { echo "validate.sh SUMMARY regressed from 11 passed, 0 failed"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: NON-GOAL enforcement: all five ecosystem/*.md files (the module index plus the four shipped protocols) are byte-identical to HEAD -- no protocol-body or module-index edit was made.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- ecosystem/README.md ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md && echo PASS || { echo "an ecosystem/*.md file was modified out of scope"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- ecosystem/; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: NON-GOAL enforcement: no schema/*.json file was modified.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- schema/ && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope schema/ file was modified"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- schema/; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: NON-GOAL enforcement: no file under tools/ besides tools/skill.json and tools/SKILL.md was modified.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- tools/ ':(exclude)tools/skill.json' ':(exclude)tools/SKILL.md' && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope tools/ file was modified"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- tools/ ':(exclude)tools/skill.json' ':(exclude)tools/SKILL.md'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: NON-GOAL enforcement: docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, docs/planning/DEBT.md, and CHANGELOG.md are byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- docs/planning/manifest.json docs/planning/ROADMAP.md docs/planning/DEBT.md CHANGELOG.md && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope planning document or CHANGELOG.md was modified"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: .agent/ immutability guard (two-part invariant, per 038 amendment_2 precedent, glob range covering the current phase-005 in-flight window 034-045): (a) no TRACKED/HISTORICAL .agent/qa or .agent/contracts record was modified; (b) every UNTRACKED addition under .agent/qa or .agent/contracts is confined to a legitimately in-flight phase-005 feature (034-045) or its review artifacts.
verification.command: bad=0; if ! git diff --quiet HEAD -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts; then echo "tracked/historical .agent/qa or .agent/contracts record modified:"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts; bad=1; fi; while IFS= read -r line; do case "$line" in "?? "*) path="${line:3}"; case "$path" in .agent/contracts/03[4-9]*.json|.agent/contracts/04[0-5]*.json|.agent/qa/03[4-9]*.json|.agent/qa/04[0-5]*.json) ;; *) echo "out-of-scope untracked .agent/qa or .agent/contracts artifact: $path"; bad=1 ;; esac ;; esac; done < <(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts); if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo PASS; else exit 1; fi

verification.acceptance_test: Scope guard (untracked-aware, concurrency-tolerant): only this feature's own three modified files, plus the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md (tolerated, not authorized to be touched), changed outside .agent/.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'NIZAM.json' 'tools/skill.json' 'tools/SKILL.md' 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md' && echo PASS || { echo "out-of-scope change(s) detected"; exit 1; }

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/040-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/040-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/040-verify-26.txt for the twenty-sixth -- the 'verification' array has exactly 26 entries). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.

approvals.round0_disposition: REJECTED (single HIGH finding: the original three-legged router-not-mirror proof for verification entries 12/14/15 -- content-completeness, verbatim-canary mirror-prevention, 40-line aggregate cap -- was shown gameable by a substantive, non-verbatim paraphrase of all four protocols' operative rules, measured at 25 added lines, close in order of magnitude to the honest label-only design's own line count; an aggregate line-count cap cannot structurally separate 'router' from 'mirror' content at any threshold wide enough to also admit the honest design. FIX in revision 1: inserted a new structural per-bullet anti-paraphrase verification entry requiring each of the four routing bullets to be exactly one physical markdown line matching '- `<path>` -- <label>' with no wrapped/continuation line following it, and <label> capped at 6 words AND 40 characters -- independent of aggregate line count. Re-proven on the disposable clone against three fixtures: the honest design PASSES; a dedicated paraphrase-mirror fixture (30 added lines, same order of magnitude as the evaluator's own 25-line fixture) correctly FAILS on all four bullets; a minimal two-physical-line stretch of a single otherwise-honest bullet correctly FAILS. The pre-existing verbatim-canary and 40-line-cap entries are retained, re-labeled as defense-in-depth, per explicit orchestrator instruction. Also corrected, per the review's separate unsupported_claims finding: design_notes.dry_run_proof's claim that the four canary sentences are unique repository-wide was false for one of the four (also present in docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md) and is now stated accurately as unique-among-the-ecosystem-protocol-bodies and absent-from-tools/SKILL.md. status remains 'proposed'; estimated_lines and files_create/files_modify/non_goals scope are unchanged by this revision.
approvals.round1_disposition: REJECTED by BOTH gates (Validator Mode A and the evaluator's round-2/revision-1 review, .agent/qa/040-contract-review-rev1.json), independently converging on the same root cause: revision 1's structural per-bullet check (entry 13) inspected only the four bullet lines and their immediate continuation line, leaving every OTHER line in the new Section 8 completely uninspected. Both gates independently built a free-standing prose paragraph placed immediately after the four (otherwise honest, unmodified) bullets -- Mode A's fixture at 22 net added lines, the evaluator's at 18 -- restating all four protocols' operative rules in ordinary sentences, reusing no exact canary sentence; both fixtures passed entries 12 (content-completeness), 13 (the prior per-bullet check, since the four bullets themselves were untouched), 15 (verbatim canary), and 16 (40-line cap) simultaneously. The evaluator separately found a MEDIUM-severity gap: entry 13 (revision 1) validated only that the four expected bullets were themselves structurally sound, never that the bullet list contained ONLY those four -- an unauthorized fifth bullet (e.g. ecosystem/README.md, which non_goals explicitly forbids registering) passed unflagged. FIX in revision 2: entry 13 was widened from a per-bullet-only check into a whole-section CLOSED-SET check -- every non-blank line in Section 8 must be either a bounded preamble (<=2 physical lines, each <=90 characters), or one of the four contracted bullets in the contracted order, with nothing else permitted anywhere in the section (before, between, or after the bullets) -- plus an explicit exact-cardinality assertion (exactly 4 bullets, exactly the 4 contracted paths, no duplicates, no extras). Section 8 itself was also redesigned (the honest tools/SKILL.md edit) to remove the prior revision's trailing 'Read ecosystem/README.md ...' closing sentence entirely, so the section now naturally has nothing after the bullets to begin with, in addition to being structurally enforced. Re-proven on a fresh disposable clone at the real, current, committed HEAD 3aed5d1 against six required scenarios (see dry_run_proof revision-2 addendum): (a) honest design passes; (b) Mode A's free-prose shape fails; (c) the evaluator's shorter variant fails; (d) a fifth bullet fails (exact-cardinality); (e) a stretched bullet with a bloated label fails on all three grounds; (f) an over-count and an over-length preamble line each fail. The pre-existing verbatim-canary and 40-line-cap entries (now 15 and 16) are retained as defense-in-depth, unchanged. status remains 'proposed'; scope, files_modify (purpose text for tools/SKILL.md updated to describe the closed-set/bounded-preamble design), and estimated_lines (95) otherwise unchanged by this revision, apart from the added closed-set non_goal.
approvals.round2_disposition: REJECTED by the evaluator alone (Validator Mode A APPROVED revision 2); round-2 review at .agent/qa/040-contract-review-rev2.json. Root cause: revision 2's whole-section closed-set fix was itself demonstrated sound for the bullet region, but its own bounded preamble field (<=2 lines, <=90 characters each) was a length-only-constrained free-text canvas -- two independently-constructed 2-line digests (170 and 157 characters) synthesized all four protocols' operative rules together, one reproducing the literal preflight verdict enum tokens PASS/PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS/FAIL verbatim, and both passed entries 12/13/15/16 simultaneously. A related, lower-severity missing_acceptance_coverage finding: the preamble's required positive content (naming ecosystem/README.md, asserting router-not-mirror) was asserted only in prose, never independently verified. ORCHESTRATOR RULING (recorded in run_state.json, per the evaluator's own circuit-breaker flag): revision 3 authorized as the FINAL round under the attempt-3 architectural-review strategy; any further gate rejection means BLOCKED + DEBT.md + human escalation, no attempt 4. FIX in revision 3 (architectural, not another bound): eliminated free text from the preamble entirely -- pinned it to an exact, byte-for-byte two-line string (design_notes.pinned_preamble_text), which itself names ecosystem/README.md and asserts the router role, so the byte-compare mechanism simultaneously closes both the smuggling vector and the required-positive-content gap. Everything else in the closed set (header, exactly 4 contracted bullets in order, 6-word/40-char label caps, nothing else) is unchanged from revision 2. Re-proven on a fresh disposable clone at the real, current, committed HEAD 3aed5d1 against five required scenarios (see dry_run_proof revision-3 addendum): (a) honest design with the pinned preamble passes; (b) both of the evaluator's own 170-char and 157-char preamble-digest fixtures fail (byte-compare mismatch); (c) a one-character preamble deviation fails; (d) all previously-closed shapes (trailing paragraph, fresh between-bullets interleave, fifth bullet, stretched bullet + bloated label) still fail; (e) the pinned preamble text itself is confirmed to contain 'ecosystem/README.md' and 'router'. status remains 'proposed'; scope, estimated_lines (95), and the verification array's length (26 entries) are otherwise unchanged by this revision -- only entry 13's command, the tools/SKILL.md purpose text, the closed-set non_goal's wording, and design_notes were revised.
